items.title: Scientists found a surprising problem with sugar-free diets
items.summary: A surprising new study suggests that completely eliminating sugar may backfire. Mice on a sucrose-free low-fat diet showed worse blood sugar control, increased inflammation, disrupted gut bacteria, and signs of fatty liver compared with mice that consumed some sucrose. items.title: People taking GLP-1 weight loss drugs like Ozempic started moving less
items.summary: People taking popular weight-loss drugs such as Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, and Zepbound may be losing pounds, but they could also be moving less. items.title: Why middle age is becoming a breaking point in the U.S.
items.summary: A new international study finds that middle-aged Americans are lonelier, more depressed, and experiencing worse memory and health than earlier generations. items.title: Your brain can keep improving into your 90s, study finds
items.summary: A three-year study of nearly 4,000 adults ranging from age 19 to 94 found that brain health can improve at any age, challenging the common belief that mental sharpness must decline as we get older.
